     The Numbers Business Report

R.I.P.D. (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $33,618,855Details
International Box Office $45,457,823
Worldwide Box Office $79,076,678
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,880,626
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $8,507,498
Total $16,388,124
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Veteran sheriff Roy Pulsifer has spent his career with the legendary police force known as R.I.P.D. tracking monstrous spirits who are cleverly disguised as ordinary people. His mission? To arrest and bring to justice a special brand of criminals trying to escape final judgment by hiding among the unsuspecting on Earth. Once Roy is assigned former rising-star detective Nick Walker as his junior officer, the new partners have to turn grudging respect into top-notch teamwork. When they uncover a plot that could end life as we know it, two of R.I.P.D.’s finest must miraculously restore the cosmic balance… or watch the tunnel to the afterlife begin sending angry souls the very wrong way.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,691,415 (37.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.65 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:42.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$130,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,852 opening theaters/2,852 max. theaters, 3.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $49,456,526

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 19th, 2013 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: October 4th, 2013 (Wide) (Mexico)
November 28th, 2013 (Wide) (Uruguay)
Video Release: October 29th, 2013 by Universal Home Entertainment
January 20th, 2014 by Universal Pictures UK, released as R.I.P.D. Rest in Peace Department
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence, sci-fi/fantasy action, some sensuality, and language including sex references.
(Rating bulletin 2268, 4/16/2013)
Running Time: 98 minutes
Franchise: R.I.P.D
Cast, crew, or production detail: Motion Capture Performance
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion
Plot point: Buddy Cop, Friendly Ghost, Haunting
Social setting: End of the World
Source material: Dark Horse Comics
Subgenre: Action Comedy
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Original Film, Dark Horse Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Veteran sheriff Roy Pulsifer has spent his career with the legendary police force known as R.I.P.D. tracking monstrous spirits who are cleverly disguised as ordinary people. His mission? To arrest and bring to justice a special brand of criminals trying to escape final judgment by hiding among the unsuspecting on Earth. Once Roy is assigned former rising-star detective Nick Walker as his junior officer, the new partners have to turn grudging respect into top-notch teamwork. When they uncover a plot that could end life as we know it, two of R.I.P.D.’s finest must miraculously restore the cosmic balance… or watch the tunnel to the afterlife begin sending angry souls the very wrong way.
